name: Herb Roasted Rabbit and Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 4 lb Rabbit [cut into 8 pieces, Rinsed & patted dry]
4 oz Slab bacon [rind removed and cut into 1" cubes]
6 tbsp Fresh rosemary leaves OR 2 tbsp Dried rosemary
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
2. Place the potatoes and garlic cloves in a large shallow roasting pan. Drizzle with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, and toss to coat. Bake for 30 minutes.
3. While the potatoes and garlic are roasting, combine the bacon and 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large skillet. Heat over low heat until the bacon begins to wilt. Remove the bacon with a slotted spoon, and set it aside.
4. Sauté the rabbit, in batches if necessary, in the skillet, setting the pieces aside as they are browned. Reserve 2 tablespoons of the pan drippings.
5. Remove the roasting pan from the oven, and reduce the heat to 350°F.
6. Add the rabbit, rosemary, pepper, coarse salt, reserved pan drippings, and remaining 3 tablespoons oil to the roasting pan with the potatoes and garlic. Toss thoroughly, and return the pan to the oven. Bake for 20 minutes.
7. Sprinkle the reserved bacon over the top, and bake until the meat is tender and the vegetables are golden, another 20 minutes.
8. Arrange the mixture on a warmed platter, and garnish with the rosemary sprigs.
